symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable vomiting with inability to retain oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting is a recognised feature of an intense acute stress response.
Sustained vomiting with no fluid retention is the direct driver of volume depletion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute surgical abdomen / acute intra-abdominal event
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting is non-specific and cannot distinguish functional from surgical cause without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute epigastric pain, worse with retching

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute surgical abdomen / acute intra-abdominal event
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute epigastric pain mandates exclusion of an intra-abdominal cause; the rule-out is a hands-on examination telehealth cannot provide.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical epigastric pain from repeated forceful retching is consistent with the functional picture, but only once organic causes are examined.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt onset immediately on exposure to a specific emotional/environmental cue

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptom onset locked precisely to a discrete emotional trigger is characteristic of a cue-driven acute stress response.
A trigger-locked onset without an ingestion exposure, diarrhoea or fever argues against gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperventilation, chest tightness and hand tremor

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation with peripheral tremor reflects respiratory alkalosis and adrenergic surge typical of an acute panic/stress crisis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute coronary syndrome / cardiac cause of chest tightness
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness is non-specific; low pretest probability here but not excludable remotely without an ECG.
Lightheadedness with inability to stand after prolonged vomiting suggests possible volume depletion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Faintness also occurs with hyperventilation-driven hypocapnia during a panic crisis.
Absence of blood or coffee-ground material lowers the probability of an active upper-GI bleed, though it does not exclude other surgical causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended, non-peritonitic abdomen with active bowel sounds (epigastric tenderness mechanical only)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute surgical abdomen / acute intra-abdominal event
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft, non-peritonitic abdomen strongly argues against a surgical abdomen — but this is a hands-on examination finding the AI Doctor CANNOT obtain, so it remains sealed and the AI must arrange the exam in person.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia and tachypnoea with normal oxygenation and no fever fit an autonomic stress crisis — but these are clinician-measured vitals the AI cannot obtain without a device; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes / renal function (if dehydration from persistent vomiting)

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible mild hypokalaemia / metabolic alkalosis or raised urea if volume-depleted; may be normal
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Electrolyte disturbance from intractable vomiting could be missed — an in-person indication, not a telehealth-obtainable result.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies volume/electrolyte impact of sustained vomiting; obtainable only in person.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute stress / panic crisis with psychogenic vomiting
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Trigger-locked onset plus hyperventilation, tremor, faintness and tachycardia form a coherent adrenergic stress-response cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only way to exclude a surgical abdomen; the telehealth service arranges it rather than performing it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Vital signs and hydration assessment in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective heart rate, blood pressure and volume status cannot be measured over chat and are needed given intractable vomiting.
Assesses electrolyte derangement from persistent vomiting if it continues.
Reasonable in person given chest tightness and tachycardia; not remotely obtainable and not to be over-ordered by the AI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal imaging (CT/US) ordered remotely by the AI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Imaging decisions follow an in-person examination; the AI should not over-order imaging from a chat encounter.
